summary: Re-validated 3 previously flagged items. All 3 mirages resolved — no critical or major findings remain.

verified_claims: MongoClient (com.mongodb.reactivestreams.client.MongoClient) — correct class for @MockBean in Spring Boot 3.5.x reactive MongoDB auto-config; ReactiveMongoClient no longer appears anywhere in the tech-spec
Spring Boot 3.5.x — all references updated (Solution, Decision 1, Task 2, User-Spec Deviations); 3.5.x is the current actively-supported 3.x line as of May 2026
compatibilityDate — wording changed to 'strongly recommended' in both Decision 2 and Task 3; no longer stated as 'required'

item_details.notes: All occurrences in the tech-spec (line 48, Decision 6 line 88, Risks table line 175) now use 'MongoClient (com.mongodb.reactivestreams.client.MongoClient)'. No bare ReactiveMongoClient remains. The class is correct: Spring Boot 3.5.x MongoReactiveAutoConfiguration creates a com.mongodb.reactivestreams.client.MongoClient bean — @MockBean on that type suppresses the connection attempt.

All references updated: Solution section, Architecture section, Decision 1 title and rationale, Task 2 description, User-Spec Deviations. Rationale 'Boot 3.5.x is the stable LTS line' is accurate as of May 2026 — 3.4.x is EOL, 3.5.x is the current supported 3.x release.

Decision 2 (line 65) and Task 3 description (line 223) both now read 'strongly recommended'. Wording accurately reflects Nuxt 4 behavior: the field is generated by nuxi init by default and controls Nitro preset behavior, but its absence does not prevent build or runtime.
